question:
Will Bitcoin dip to $90,000 in November?

Description: This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

evidences.title: Top Market Events in November 2025: From FOMC to x402 Protocol ...
evidences.content: ### Liquidity and Flow Factors ETF Flows: BlackRock’s IBIT remains the biggest driver of U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF inflows. Consistent inflows through November will be key to maintaining upward momentum in BTC. Derivatives Expiry: Both Deribit options and CME Bitcoin futures expire on Nov 28 at 08:00 UTC, during the U.S. Thanksgiving week. Lower liquidity could lead to sharper price swings around that date. ### Narrative Watch [...] ## November Calendar At-a-Glance ### Macro highlights | | | | --- | Date | Event | Why It Matters | | Nov 6 | Bank of England Meeting | Sets tone for UK monetary policy | | Nov 7 | U.S. Jobs Report | First full labor data after shutdown | | Nov 13–14 | U.S. CPI & PPI | Core inflation guide for Fed’s December move | | Nov 19 | FOMC Minutes | Signals how confident the Fed is about more cuts | | Nov 20 | China Loan Prime Rate | Benchmark for credit and yuan stability | | Nov 21 | Eurozone PMIs | Measure of growth recovery | | Nov 26 | Core PCE Price Index (US) | Fed’s preferred inflation gauge; shapes rate-cut expectations | | Nov 26 | UK Autumn Budget | Fiscal clues for 2026 | | Nov 30 | OPEC+ JMMC | Determines oil supply path into 2026 | ### Crypto highli...

evidences.title: What To Expect From Bitcoin Price In November 2025
evidences.content: > "Global trade tensions, inflation, and recession fears have weighed heavily on all risk assets, and Bitcoin hasn't been spared. We've seen it trade in that $104,000 to $108,000 range recently. Looking ahead, I think November will likely bring consolidation or a modest recovery — not a full-on rally unless a strong catalyst appears. If trade tensions worsen, Bitcoin could retest the $90,000 area. But if support holds above $110,000, we could easily see a 10 to 20% rebound toward $120,000 to $140,000 by the end of the month, especially with ETF inflows holding up and whales quietly accumulating," stated Lin. [...] Bitcoin Price Analysis. Source: TradingView However, if Bitcoin fails to sustain momentum above $115,000, short-term weakness could reemerge. A dip toward $110,000 remains possible if buyers lose conviction. Any move below this support would invalidate the bullish outlook. Read original story What To Expect From Bitcoin Price In November 2025 by Aaryamann Shrivastava at beincrypto.com Terms and Privacy Policy Privacy Dashboard More Info ## Recommended Stories [...] ## Bitcoin Is Establishing Crucial Levels On-chain data adds another layer of insight into Bitcoin's curr...

evidences.title: MicroStrategy Stock and Bitcoin 2025: Dive into Bitcoin's Impact
evidences.content: MicroStrategy has made headlines with its bold investment strategy in Bitcoin. Consequently, investors are wondering about MicroStrategy’s stock predictions for 2025. Predictions for MicroStrategy stock in 2025 show high variability. Bitcoin holdings heavily influence them. MarketBeat suggests a price range from $409 to $650. The average is $536.90. Stock Analysis forecasts an average target price of $382.10, with a potential reach of up to $650. Analysts are generally optimistic about MicroStrategy’s future performance in 2025. The company’s aggressive Bitcoin investments have a significant impact on its stock predictions. MicroStrategy’s pricing is closely tied to the volatile cryptocurrency market. Since starting Bitcoin acquisitions in 2020, the MicroStrategy chart has shown a 2169% [...] ### Insights from Industry Experts Analysts remain divided on MicroStrategy’s prospects. evidences.title: BlackRock vs Fidelity: Who Controls the Future of Bitcoin ...
evidences.content: ### Assets Under Management Compared Out front in the Bitcoin ETF race sits BlackRock’s IBIT. Years rolled by before BlackRock’s entry climbed to dominate spot offerings, swelling past dozens of billions. Close behind trails Fidelity’s FBTC – large, yet still short of the leader. When a fund grows big, narrower gaps between buy and sell prices tend to follow, along with stronger trading activity. ### ETF Money Flow and What Investors Want in 2026 Nowadays, money moving into Bitcoin ETFs acts like a pulse check for the market. When these flows grow, it often means financial advisors, investment firms, wealthy families, or broker customers are buying in. On days when cash pulls out, it can point to caution or fading trust. [...] ### Can Bitcoin ETF Inflows Cause a Supply Shock? True, these factors might tighten Bitcoin availability should buying continue alongside shrinking market liquidity. Still, that outcome isn’t certain. ### What Makes One Bitcoin ETF Stand Out From Others by 2026? Out front, when it comes to cash flow, IBIT also holds more assets than others, plus grabs a bigger slice of trading volume. For those already using Fidelity’s tools, FBTC fits naturally into their...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Prediction 2025: Analyzing the Next Moves for BTC/USDT Amid Regulatory, Political, and On-Chain Tailwinds
evidences.content: ## Technical Analysis of BTC/USDT Now, let's analyze the technical aspects of Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) to anticipate its potential direction in the upcoming weeks and months. As of July 2025, Bitcoin has experienced a strong uptrend, with key price movements reflecting consolidation below the critical resistance of $110K–$112K. Trend Analysis & Chart Structure: On higher time frames, BTC/USDT is in an established uptrend, marked by higher highs and lows since the 2022 bear market. The 2024 halving propelled Bitcoin to new all-time highs (ATHs) of around $111-112K. Currently, the price is consolidating between $100K and $110K, with multiple rejections around the $110K level forming a potential triple top pattern. KeyResistance Levels: [...] Stock-to-Flow (S2F): This controversial model anticipates prices surpassing $100K post-2024 halving, with some estimates as high as $288K. Logarithmic Regression Bands: These suggest a slower but consistent upward trend, with late 2025 targets around $200K-$250K. Fibonacci Extensions: Using historical lows and highs, targets range from $139K to around $171K based on different calculations. Mayer Multiple or MVRV extremes: Historically, Bitcoin price...

evidences.content: Welcome to Plan B on YouTube. So, Bitcoin closed October at $109,000. And that is the sixth month in a row that it's above 100k. So, we really see this 100k that used to be resistance uh, turn into support. A really bullish sign. Now, from high to low, this is the stock-to-flow at 500k. This is Bitcoin at 109k. And this is the realized price at 56, moving average at 55k. So, what's next? In fact, there's a lot of people that are very bearish. And their thinking is like this. Okay, this was the halving and we're now 1 and 1/2 year uh, after the halving. And historically, on uh, a year to 1 and 1/2 year after the halving, here's another halving, uh, 1 and 1/2 year after that halving, that was historically the top. Also in 2013. So, 1 year, 1 and 1/2 year, there's a top. We're now 1 and 1/2 [...] in fact what what I what I'm looking for. And also, it's very hard to imagine a bear market, blue, without some FOMO, um, some crazy bull market. You know, these are the people that that bought here, that that that are liquidated here. evidences.title: Do Bitcoin ETFs Lead Price Discovery Following their Introduction in the Bitcoin Market? | Computational Economics | Springer Nature Link
evidences.content: ")), who identified the bitcoin spot as the main driver of price discovery vis-à-vis ETPs. We hypothesise that this contrasting result is due to the following reasons. First, the bitcoin ETPs in Gemayel’s study were traded on Eurozone exchanges (August 2021 to July 2022), while the bitcoin ETFs in this study were launched in January 2024 and regulated by the US SEC. Second, the growing popularity of Bitcoin ETFs such as IBIT, FBTC and GBTC among investors for bitcoin investments via brokerage accounts, as evidenced by their significant trading volumes following the highly anticipated US SEC approval and nine months thereafter. [...] the three most actively traded bitcoin ETFs, namely IBIT, FBTC and GBTC, dominate price discovery in terms of pure speed (ILS) over the bitcoin spot about 85 per cent of the time during the studied period. [...] Fig. 1 The most actively traded bitcoin ETFs based on trading volume as of 11 October 2024. Note: This figure shows the most active bitcoin ETFs based on a 20-day average trading volume as of 11 October 2024. The top four most actively traded bitcoin ETFs, IBIT, FBTC, GBTC, and BITB, are issued by Blackrock iShares, Fidelity Investments, Gray...

Investors also face other risks, including significant and negative price swings, flash crashes, and fraud and cybersecurity risks. Digital assets may also be more susceptible to market manipulation than securities. evidences.title: Changelog | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Websocket API `trades.aggregate` `klines` `allOrderList` `allOrders` `myTrades` Users connected to the websocket API will now be disconnected if their IP is banned due to violation of the IP rate limits (status `418`). The following changes will take effect approximately a week from the release date, but the rest of the documentation has been updated to reflect the future changes: Changes to Filter Evaluation: Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. New behavior: This has now been changed to (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Please use the following endpoints instead moving forward. USER DATA STREAM Changes to`executionReport` event If the C field is empty, it will now properly return `null`, instead of `"null"`.
